topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring intrusion in the quiet hum of my laptop. A photo. My history teacher, Mr. Harrison, grinning awkwardly next to a group of students at a recent school event. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle twitching in my cheek. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the empty room. Why? Why would he be broadcasting his life? I felt a hot flush creep up my neck, making my skin prickle. This felt like an invasion, a blatant disregard for boundaries I hadn't even known existed until this moment. I stood up, pacing the small confines of my bedroom, my fists clenching and unclenching. He thought he was untouchable, a figure of authority, but seeing him there, casually sharing moments, just made my stomach churn with sheer frustration.

[story 2]

I scrolled through my feed, my breath catching in my throat as I landed on it. Mr. Harrison. His profile picture, smiling broadly, was superimposed over a candid shot of him looking relaxed. A wave of intense irritation washed over me, making my hands tremble slightly as I gripped my phone. It was so transparent, this public display. I reread the caption, a tight knot forming in my chest. He was documenting everything, curating an image of effortless connection. I wanted to shout, to confront him, but the words caught in my throat, replaced by a burning sensation behind my eyes. I threw the phone onto my bedspread, the impact barely registering against the rising tide of resentment. This was unprofessional, a complete overstep.

[story 3]

The screen illuminated my face, and I couldn't look away. It was Mr. Harrison, looking far too pleased with himself, tagged in a post about a recent school project. A deep, simmering heat began to build in my chest, a pressure that made my breathing shallow. I stared at the image, my knuckles white where I held my tablet. Every pixel seemed to mock my privacy. I felt a sudden, sharp need to lash out, to dismantle this carefully constructed facade of mentorship he presented to the world. I shoved the device away with unnecessary force, the movement jerky and uncontrolled. The silence in the room was heavy, saturated with a profound sense of being disregarded.
